name: GitHub MCP Server
description: Access GitHub repos, issues, PRs, and actions directly from Claude Code.
longDescription: The official GitHub MCP server gives Claude Code full access to your GitHub repositories. Create and manage issues, review pull requests, trigger workflows, search code, and manage branches - all without leaving your terminal. Supports both personal and organisation repos with fine-grained token permissions.
author: Anthropic

name: Filesystem MCP Server
description: Secure file system access with configurable permissions and sandboxing.
longDescription: The filesystem MCP server provides Claude Code with controlled access to your local file system. Configure allowed directories, set read/write permissions, and enable safe file operations. Essential for any workflow that needs to read configs, write output files, or manage project structures outside the current working directory.
author: Anthropic

name: Slack MCP Server
description: Read and send Slack messages, search channels, and manage conversations.
longDescription: Connect Claude Code to your Slack workspace. Search messages across channels, read conversation threads, send messages and drafts, and manage channel membership. Useful for pulling context from team discussions into your coding workflow, or automating notifications when tasks complete.
author: Anthropic

name: Google Drive MCP Server
description: Search and read files from Google Drive, Docs, and Sheets.
longDescription: Access your Google Drive files directly from Claude Code. Search across documents, read Google Docs and Sheets content, and pull file metadata. Great for referencing specs, design docs, or data stored in Google Workspace without switching context.
author: Anthropic

name: Puppeteer MCP Server
description: Browser automation - navigate pages, take screenshots, extract data.
longDescription: Run a headless browser from Claude Code. Navigate to URLs, interact with page elements, take screenshots, and extract structured data from web pages. Perfect for testing web apps, scraping reference data, or debugging frontend issues directly from the terminal.
author: Anthropic

name: PostgreSQL MCP Server
description: Query PostgreSQL databases, inspect schemas, and run migrations.
longDescription: Connect Claude Code directly to your PostgreSQL databases. Run queries, inspect table schemas, list databases and tables, and execute migrations. Supports connection strings and SSL. Invaluable for debugging data issues or writing queries without leaving your development flow.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: claude mcp add postgres -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb

name: SQLite MCP Server
description: Read and write SQLite databases with full SQL support.
longDescription: Lightweight database access for Claude Code. Open any SQLite database file, run queries, create tables, and inspect schemas. Ideal for local development databases, prototyping, or working with embedded database files in your projects.
author: Anthropic

name: Brave Search MCP Server
description: Web search via Brave's API - find docs, examples, and solutions.
longDescription: Add web search capabilities to Claude Code through Brave's search API. Look up documentation, find code examples, research error messages, and discover solutions - all from within your terminal session. Requires a Brave Search API key.
author: Anthropic

name: Memory MCP Server
description: Persistent knowledge graph for storing entities, relations, and context across sessions.
longDescription: Give Claude Code a persistent memory that survives across sessions. Store entities (people, projects, concepts), create relations between them, and build a knowledge graph that grows over time. Uses a local JSON file for storage. Essential for long-running projects where context continuity matters.
author: Anthropic

name: Fetch MCP Server
description: Make HTTP requests to any URL - fetch APIs, download content, test endpoints.
longDescription: A simple but powerful MCP server that lets Claude Code make HTTP requests. Fetch web pages, call REST APIs, download files, and test endpoints. Converts HTML to markdown for readability. Essential for any workflow that needs to pull data from the web.
author: Anthropic

name: Sequential Thinking MCP Server
description: Structured step-by-step reasoning for complex problem solving.
longDescription: Adds a tool that enables Claude Code to think through problems step by step in a structured way. Each thought builds on previous ones, with the ability to revise and branch. Particularly useful for debugging complex issues, architectural decisions, and multi-step reasoning where you want to see the thought process laid out clearly.
author: Anthropic

name: Sentry MCP Server
description: Access Sentry errors, stack traces, and issue data from your terminal.
longDescription: Pull production error data directly into Claude Code. Search and retrieve Sentry issues, view stack traces, check error frequency, and get event details. Speeds up debugging by letting Claude analyse real errors alongside your code without context switching to the Sentry dashboard.
author: Sentry

name: Linear MCP Server
description: Manage Linear issues, projects, and cycles from Claude Code.
longDescription: Integrate Linear project management into your Claude Code workflow. Create and update issues, manage project cycles, search across your workspace, and link code changes to tickets. Keeps your development flow connected to your project management without browser tab switching.
author: Linear

name: Cloudflare MCP Server
description: Manage Cloudflare Workers, KV, R2, and D1 from the terminal.
longDescription: Deploy and manage Cloudflare infrastructure directly from Claude Code. Create and update Workers, manage KV namespaces, interact with R2 storage buckets, and query D1 databases. Useful for serverless workflows where you want Claude to handle deployment alongside development.
author: Cloudflare

name: Stripe MCP Server
description: Access Stripe payments data, customers, and subscriptions.
longDescription: Query your Stripe account from Claude Code. Look up payments, manage customers, check subscription statuses, and retrieve invoice data. Helpful for debugging payment flows or pulling business metrics into your development context.
author: Stripe

name: Docker MCP Server
description: Manage Docker containers, images, and compose stacks from Claude Code.
longDescription: Control your Docker environment without leaving Claude Code. List and manage containers, build and pull images, inspect logs, manage Docker Compose stacks, and handle networking. Lets Claude help you debug containerised applications by inspecting running services directly.
author: Docker

name: Notion MCP Server
description: Search and read Notion pages, databases, and workspace content.
longDescription: Connect your Notion workspace to Claude Code. Search across pages and databases, read page content, and query database entries. Useful for pulling specs, notes, or documentation from Notion into your coding context without switching applications.
author: Notion

name: /commit Skill
description: Smart git commits - analyses changes and writes meaningful commit messages.
longDescription: The built-in /commit skill analyses your staged and unstaged changes, understands the intent behind modifications, and creates well-structured commit messages following conventional commit format. Handles multi-file changes gracefully and can be customised via CLAUDE.md instructions.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: # Built-in skill, just type /commit in Claude Code

name: /review-pr Skill
description: Automated PR review - checks code quality, bugs, and suggests improvements.
longDescription: Invoke /review-pr to get a thorough code review of any pull request. Analyses the diff, checks for bugs, security issues, performance problems, and style inconsistencies. Provides actionable feedback with specific line references. Works with GitHub PRs via the GitHub MCP server or local diffs.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: # Built-in skill, just type /review-pr in Claude Code

name: /create-pr Skill
description: Creates GitHub pull requests with auto-generated titles and descriptions.
longDescription: The /create-pr skill handles the full PR creation workflow. It analyses all commits on your branch, generates a clear title and description summarising the changes, pushes to remote if needed, and creates the PR via the GitHub CLI. Follows repository conventions and includes a test plan.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: # Built-in skill, just type /create-pr in Claude Code

name: Pre-commit Lint Hook
description: Automatically lint and format code before every commit Claude makes.
longDescription: A Claude Code hook that runs your project's linter and formatter before every commit. Configure it to run ESLint, Prettier, Black, Ruff, or any other tool. Catches formatting issues before they make it into your git history. Integrates with the /commit skill seamlessly.
author: Community

name: Test on File Change Hook
description: Automatically runs relevant tests when Claude modifies source files.
longDescription: This hook watches for file modifications by Claude Code and automatically runs related test files. If Claude edits src/utils/parser.ts, it will run src/utils/parser.test.ts. Supports Jest, Vitest, Pytest, and Go test conventions. Catches regressions instantly during development.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add to .claude/hooks.json - see repo docs for configuration

name: Task Completion Notification Hook
description: Get desktop/Slack notifications when long-running Claude tasks finish.
longDescription: Configure notifications for when Claude Code completes a task. Supports macOS native notifications, Slack webhooks, and custom shell commands. Particularly useful when you kick off a long task and switch to something else - you'll know the moment it's done.
author: Community
installCommand:
# Add to .claude/hooks.json:
# {"on-complete": ["osascript -e 'display notification \"Task done\" with title \"Claude Code\"'"]}

name: CLAUDE.md Project Memory
description: Project-level instructions that persist across Claude Code sessions.
longDescription: The CLAUDE.md file is Claude Code's project memory. Place it in your repo root to give Claude persistent context about your project: coding conventions, architecture decisions, deployment procedures, and custom instructions. Supports global (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md), project-level, and directory-specific files that stack together.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: # Create a CLAUDE.md file in your project root

name: Lessons Learned Pattern
description: Track mistakes and corrections so Claude never repeats them.
longDescription: A memory pattern where you maintain a lessons.md file that Claude reads at session start. After any correction from the user, Claude adds the pattern to this file. Over time, it builds a personalised knowledge base of what works and what doesn't for your specific projects and preferences.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add to CLAUDE.md: "After any correction, update tasks/lessons.md with the pattern"

name: Tech Stack Memory File
description: Document your stack so Claude always knows your tools and versions.
longDescription: Create a structured section in CLAUDE.md that documents your technology stack: languages, frameworks, package managers, deployment targets, and version constraints. This prevents Claude from suggesting incompatible solutions or outdated patterns. Include versions, preferred libraries, and anti-patterns to avoid.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add a ## Tech Stack section to your CLAUDE.md

name: Subagent Delegation Workflow
description: Use subagents to parallelize research and keep your main context clean.
longDescription: A workflow pattern for using Claude Code subagents effectively. Delegate research, code exploration, and parallel analysis tasks to subagents while keeping your main conversation focused. Each subagent gets a focused task and returns results. Key rule: MCP tool calls must stay in the main agent context.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add subagent rules to your CLAUDE.md - see repo for examples

name: Test-Driven Development Workflow
description: Configure Claude to always write tests first, then implementation.
longDescription: Set up Claude Code to follow strict TDD practices. Configure CLAUDE.md instructions that require Claude to write failing tests before implementation, run tests after every change, and maintain coverage thresholds. Works with any test framework and ensures your codebase stays well-tested.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add TDD rules to your CLAUDE.md - see repo for examples

name: Deploy and Verify Workflow
description: Automated deployment with post-deploy health checks and rollback.
longDescription: A structured workflow pattern for deployments. Claude builds, runs tests, deploys to staging, runs health checks, then promotes to production. Includes rollback steps if health checks fail. Can be configured for Fly.io, Vercel, AWS, or any deployment target via CLAUDE.md instructions.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add deployment workflow rules to your CLAUDE.md

name: EverArt MCP Server
description: Generate and manage AI images using various models from the terminal.
longDescription: Create AI-generated images directly from Claude Code using EverArt's model library. Generate images with text prompts, manage styles, and download results. Useful for generating placeholder images, marketing assets, or visual prototypes without leaving your development workflow.
author: EverArt

name: Redis MCP Server
description: Connect to Redis for caching, pub/sub, and key-value operations.
longDescription: Access Redis directly from Claude Code. Get and set keys, manage TTLs, work with hashes, lists and sets, subscribe to channels, and inspect memory usage. Useful for debugging cache issues, testing pub/sub flows, or managing session data during development.
author: Community
installCommand: claude mcp add redis -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-redis redis://localhost:6379

name: Iterative Code Review Loop
description: Claude reviews its own code changes and iterates until quality bar is met.
longDescription: A workflow pattern where Claude Code reviews its own changes before committing. After writing code, it switches to a review mindset: checking for bugs, edge cases, performance issues, and style violations. If issues are found, it fixes them and reviews again. Continues until the quality bar is met. Set the bar in CLAUDE.md.
author: Community
installCommand: # Add self-review rules to your CLAUDE.md - see repo for examples

name: Security Scan Hook
description: Scan for secrets, vulnerabilities, and security issues before committing.
longDescription: A pre-commit hook that runs security checks on Claude's code changes. Scans for hardcoded secrets, API keys, common vulnerability patterns, and unsafe code. Uses tools like gitleaks, trufflehog, or custom regex patterns. Prevents accidental secret leaks and catches security issues early.
author: Community

name: Google Calendar MCP Server
description: Manage calendar events, check availability, and schedule meetings.
longDescription: Access Google Calendar from Claude Code. List upcoming events, create and update meetings, check free/busy times, find meeting slots, and respond to invitations. Useful when you need to coordinate work schedules or block time for focused development without switching to your browser.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: claude mcp add google-calendar -- npx -y @anthropic/claude-code-mcp install google-calendar

name: Gmail MCP Server
description: Read, search, and draft emails directly from Claude Code.
longDescription: Connect Gmail to Claude Code for email management. Search messages, read threads, create drafts, and check your inbox - all from the terminal. Helpful for pulling context from email threads into your development workflow or drafting responses that reference code changes.
author: Anthropic
installCommand: claude mcp add gmail -- npx -y @anthropic/claude-code-mcp install gmail

name: Notion API
description: Create, read, and update Notion pages and databases via REST API.
longDescription: The Notion API gives you full programmatic access to your Notion workspace. Create and update pages, query databases with filters and sorts, manage blocks, and search content. Claude Code can call it directly via curl or the official SDK. Offers more granular control than the MCP server - you can build complex database queries, batch-update properties, and handle pagination yourself.
author: Notion

name: Linear API
description: GraphQL API for managing Linear issues, projects, cycles, and teams.
longDescription: Linear's GraphQL API gives you complete access to your project management data. Create and update issues, manage cycles, query project states, and automate workflows. The GraphQL schema is well-documented and the SDK handles authentication and pagination. More flexible than the MCP server for custom queries and bulk operations.
author: Linear

name: GitHub REST API
description: Full GitHub access - repos, issues, PRs, actions, packages, and more.
longDescription: The GitHub REST API (and GraphQL API) provides complete access to every GitHub feature. Create repos, manage issues and PRs, trigger workflows, query commit history, manage releases, and handle webhooks. Octokit is the official SDK with excellent TypeScript support. Use this when you need operations the MCP server doesn't cover, or when building custom automation scripts.
author: GitHub

name: Slack Web API
description: Send messages, manage channels, upload files, and automate Slack workflows.
longDescription: The Slack Web API gives full control over your Slack workspace. Post messages with rich formatting, manage channels and users, upload files, set reminders, and build interactive workflows. The Bolt SDK simplifies common patterns. More powerful than the MCP server for complex message formatting, interactive components, and event handling.
author: Slack

name: Google Drive API
description: Upload, download, search, and manage files in Google Drive programmatically.
longDescription: The Google Drive API provides full file management capabilities. Upload and download files, create folders, set permissions, search across your Drive, and convert between formats. Supports resumable uploads for large files and real-time change notifications. More control than the MCP server for file uploads, permission management, and batch operations.
author: Google

name: Google Calendar API
description: Create events, check availability, and manage calendars via REST.
longDescription: The Google Calendar API lets you create and manage events, query free/busy information, manage multiple calendars, and set up push notifications for changes. Supports recurring events, time zone handling, and attendee management. Use this over the MCP server when you need complex recurrence rules, batch event creation, or webhook-based notifications.
author: Google

name: Gmail API
description: Read, send, search, and manage email programmatically via Google's API.
longDescription: The Gmail API provides complete email management. Read and send messages, manage labels, search with Gmail's powerful query syntax, handle attachments, and set up push notifications. Supports batch operations and MIME message construction. More capable than the MCP server for sending emails with attachments, managing labels, and building email automation pipelines.
author: Google

name: Stripe API
description: Manage payments, subscriptions, customers, and invoices via Stripe's SDK.
longDescription: Stripe's API is one of the best-documented APIs in the industry. Manage payments, subscriptions, customers, invoices, refunds, and payouts. The Node SDK provides excellent TypeScript types and auto-pagination. Use this over the MCP server when you need to create charges, manage subscriptions, or build payment flows that require custom logic.
author: Stripe

name: OpenAI API
description: Access GPT models, embeddings, image generation, and audio transcription.
longDescription: The OpenAI API provides access to GPT models, DALL-E, Whisper, and embeddings. Generate text, create images, transcribe audio, and build embeddings for search. The Node SDK handles streaming, retries, and rate limiting. Useful from Claude Code when you need to call OpenAI models for specific tasks like embeddings generation or image creation as part of a larger workflow.
author: OpenAI

name: Anthropic API
description: Call Claude models directly for text generation, analysis, and tool use.
longDescription: The Anthropic API gives direct access to Claude models. Generate text, use tool calling, process images, and stream responses. The SDK handles authentication, retries, and streaming. Useful within Claude Code scripts when you need to spawn sub-tasks to other Claude instances, generate content in batch, or build pipelines that process data through Claude programmatically.
author: Anthropic

name: Vercel API
description: Deploy projects, manage domains, and check deployment status via REST.
longDescription: The Vercel REST API lets you trigger deployments, manage domains, check build logs, configure environment variables, and manage teams. Pair it with the Vercel CLI for a complete deployment workflow from Claude Code. No official SDK, but the REST API is clean and well-documented - curl calls work perfectly.
author: Vercel

name: Fly.io API
description: Deploy and manage Fly.io apps, machines, volumes, and secrets.
longDescription: The Fly.io Machines API gives you direct control over your Fly infrastructure. Create and manage machines, handle volumes, manage secrets, check app status, and scale deployments. Works well from Claude Code via the fly CLI or direct REST calls. The CLI is often easier, but the API is there when you need programmatic control in scripts.
author: Fly.io

name: Cloudflare API
description: Manage Workers, KV, R2, D1, DNS, and more via Cloudflare's REST API.
longDescription: The Cloudflare API covers the full platform: Workers, KV namespaces, R2 buckets, D1 databases, DNS records, firewall rules, and analytics. Well-documented with OpenAPI specs. Use this alongside or instead of the MCP server when you need operations it doesn't cover, like DNS management, firewall configuration, or analytics queries.
author: Cloudflare

name: Twilio API
description: Send SMS, make calls, and manage phone numbers programmatically.
longDescription: Twilio's API handles SMS, voice calls, WhatsApp messages, and phone number management. The Node SDK provides a clean interface for sending messages, making calls, and handling webhooks. Works well from Claude Code for sending notifications, building communication workflows, or testing messaging integrations.
author: Twilio

name: SendGrid API
description: Send transactional and marketing emails at scale via SendGrid.
longDescription: SendGrid's API handles transactional emails, marketing campaigns, and email analytics. Send templated emails, manage contact lists, track delivery metrics, and handle bounces. The Node SDK is straightforward for basic email sending. Useful from Claude Code when you need to send notifications, test email templates, or automate email workflows.
author: Twilio

name: Resend API
description: Modern email API built for developers - simple, fast, and reliable.
longDescription: Resend provides a clean, modern API for sending emails. Supports React Email templates, custom domains, webhooks, and detailed analytics. The SDK is minimal and well-typed. A popular choice for developers who find SendGrid over-complicated. Works great from Claude Code for quick email sends and testing email flows.
author: Resend

name: Supabase API
description: Postgres database, auth, storage, and edge functions via Supabase's SDK.
longDescription: Supabase provides a full backend-as-a-service with a great developer experience. Query your Postgres database, manage authentication, upload files to storage, and invoke edge functions. The JS client handles real-time subscriptions and row-level security. Excellent for Claude Code workflows that need to read/write application data, manage users, or test database queries.
author: Supabase

name: PlanetScale API
description: Manage PlanetScale databases, branches, and deploy requests via REST.
longDescription: The PlanetScale API lets you manage serverless MySQL databases, create and merge branches, handle deploy requests, and query connection strings. Follows a git-like branching model for database schema changes. Use from Claude Code to manage database branches alongside your code branches, or to automate schema deployments.
author: PlanetScale
